prompt: I have a personal website at example.com hosted on Nginx. My Nostr public key is b0635d6a9851d3aed0cd6c495b282167acf761729078d975fc341b22650b07b9. I want to set up NIP-05 verification so people can find me as bob@example.com. Walk me through the complete setup — the nostr.json file, the Nginx config, and what I need to update in my Nostr profile.
assertions: Output includes a nostr.json file with a 'names' object mapping 'bob' to the hex pubkey b0635d6a9851d3aed0cd6c495b282167acf761729078d975fc341b22650b07b9
The pubkey in the nostr.json is in 64-character lowercase hex format, NOT npub/bech32 format
Output includes Nginx configuration with Access-Control-Allow-Origin set to * (wildcard)
Nginx config uses exact match location (location = or location block) for /.well-known/nostr.json
Output explains that the file must be served over HTTPS, not HTTP
Output includes a kind:0 event or profile update showing the nip05 field set to bob@example.com
Output includes at least one curl command to test the endpoint or verify CORS headers
Output mentions that HTTP redirects must not occur on this endpoint
